interpretation_summary: The replication team succeeded in building the R-based Docker image, repairing path and syntax problems, re-generating the processed data set (N = 339) and running the preregistered hierarchical OLS model (Model_3).  The container exited with status-0 signaling that the code, including k17_analysis.R, executed to completion.  However, no artefact or captured console log containing the regression summary was redirected to a file, so the actual coefficient, standard error and p-value for the focal predictor (req_relax) are not available to evaluate the hypothesis.  As a consequence, the substantive comparison with the original estimate (β = .15, p < .01) cannot be performed, even though the analytic procedure itself appears faithful to the preregistration.

fidelity_assessment.method_alignment: High alignment: the executed script reproduced the preregistered data-processing pipeline and ran the exact hierarchical lm() specification (Model_3) with the stated covariates and outcome.  Minor deviations (standardising predictors with scale() vs. raw scores in the prereg plan) are immaterial for sign/p-value.

deviations.issue_description: No redirection of summary(Model_3) output to a file that the orchestrator can capture.
deviations.impact: High: prevents extraction of coefficient and p-value needed for replication assessment.

results_comparison.hypothesis_tested: Weekend relaxation at Time-2 positively predicts joviality at Time-3 controlling for baseline joviality and covariates.
results_comparison.replication_results: Not retrievable – model ran but coefficient/p-value were not saved in execution outputs.
results_comparison.overall_answer: Unable to judge: required statistics were not captured.

replication_report: Indeterminate: Code executed successfully and appears method-faithful, but absence of captured regression output means replication success with respect to the focal claim cannot be evaluated.

failure_handling.suggestions: Amend k17_analysis.R (or wrapper) to write summary(Model_3) to a .txt or .csv (e.g., broom::tidy(Model_3)) and add it to /app/artifacts so orchestrator can read it.

notes: If the coefficient were stored the replication would likely be assessable; recommend re-running with output capture.
